summary: I updated `docs/developer-guide.md` to capture the one piece of the iteration-3 design not already reflected in its existing "Adding a derived, live-broadcast status view" reference section: the **F1 tone-source-consistency contract**. The section already documented the `StatusSummary` PORO, the `_status_summary` partial/`with_board` scope, and the `BroadcastStatus` call sites, so I left those unchanged and made two focused edits — (1) in §5 I recorded that the summary's tone is never a per-branch color literal but is looked up in the shared `StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES` table `status_badge` also reads, that landing the feature required the single reconciling retone `STATUS_TONES["aborted"] :danger → :muted` (keeping red reserved for error stops and making a deliberate cancel read gray on both badge and summary), and that the same PR adds the matching "canceled/aborted → gray" row to `guides/ui-style-guide.md`; and (2) in §4 I updated the semantic-color summary so the shared convention it mirrors now shows gray covering a deliberate cancel and red reserved for `stuck/failed/blocked` error stops. I did not touch the guides themselves (that guide-table addition is part of the Build PR, not this docs step) and left everything else in the section as-is since it was already accurate.
